essence: Guides land‑use decisions by assessing environmental impacts, protecting habitats, and shaping sustainable development.

identity.title: Environmental Planner
identity.summary: Evaluates development proposals, conducts environmental assessments, and advises on sustainable land use to balance growth with environmental protection.

salary.notes: Senior planners or specialists in major infrastructure projects can exceed £70k-£85k+.

qualifications: Training in environmental planning, environmental science, or geography
Knowledge of planning policy and environmental legislation
Ability to conduct environmental impact assessments (EIAs)
Understanding of land‑use planning, habitats, and sustainability principles

lifestyle.description: Environmental planners work for councils, consultancies, environmental agencies, and infrastructure projects. They assess environmental impacts, guide planning decisions, and ensure developments protect habitats and comply with regulations. The role suits individuals who enjoy analysis, policy, sustainability, and shaping long‑term land‑use outcomes.

habits: Reviews planning applications for environmental impact
Conducts or contributes to environmental impact assessments
Advises developers and councils on sustainable land use
Balances ecological, social, and economic considerations
Engages with communities, stakeholders, and environmental bodies
